pi_items.source: Public dataset from principlesofeconometrics.com and Gretl data repository
food.csv contains 40 observations of household food expenditure and income

privileged_info_summary: The food.gdt dataset comes from the Principles of Econometrics, 5th Edition - a widely-used econometrics textbook. It contains 40 real observations of weekly household food expenditure and weekly income. The OLS regression coefficients (intercept=83.416, slope=10.2096) are verified against the actual data. The point forecasts at income levels 15, 20, and 25 (236.56, 287.61, 338.66) are correctly computed from these coefficients. The data is not synthetic - it derives from real household survey data used in econometric education. All coefficient values match the expected values to within numerical precision well below the stated tolerances.
